summary: Addressed systems_architect REQUEST_CHANGES for M3 save compatibility by making requestSaveV1 explicitly reject WorldState state.m3 before creating a save envelope, save DTO, bytes, or eventTail records. Added regression coverage for the accepted sim.create-obligation probe scenario proving M3 state and sim.obligation-created eventTail no longer produce unloadable save bytes. This keeps the fix inside allowed paths and avoids touching packages/save-format.

tests_run.result: Expected failing regression before implementation: requestSaveV1 did not throw for M3 state after accepted sim.create-obligation; 1 failed, 4 passed.
Focused M3 regression passed after implementation; 1 file passed, 5 tests passed.
Working tree contained only allowed-path M3 implementation, task/message state, and route/handoff artifacts at the start of the R2 fix.
10 files passed, 52 tests passed, including M3 save rejection regression and save-load contract tests.
Node/worker hello hash 61e0ba1c, command/query hash d275faa4, save/load hash 28074edd; deterministic save/load byte length 10920.
Architecture boundaries passed; determinism passed; 20 root test files passed with 101 tests; web test passed with 1 file and 3 tests.
ESLint passed for apps, packages, tests, tools, and root config files.
Task status set back to REVIEW after the R2 fix.
Task JSON validated in REVIEW state.

tests_run.command: node -e task JSON parse/shape check for M3-POLITY-VASSALAGE-001
tests_run.result: Task shape ok: id M3-POLITY-VASSALAGE-001, status REVIEW, route_to systems_architect, scope arrays present.
Final status shows allowed-path M3 implementation, task/message state, route artifacts, and the R2 handoff; no forbidden paths are modified.

decisions: Chose explicit requestSaveV1 rejection instead of a full M3 save path because packages/save-format is forbidden for this change request.
The guard runs before createSaveEnvelopeV1, worldStateV0ToSaveDto, encodeSaveEnvelopeV1, and domainEventToRecord so M3 state cannot produce M3-stripped save bytes or unparseable M3 eventTail records.
Existing non-M3 save/load behavior remains covered by the sim-core save-load contract tests and pnpm sim:determinism save/load canary.
risks: M3 persistence remains intentionally unsupported until a future task opens save-format scope and adds matching snapshot DTO and eventTail parsers.
Callers using requestSaveV1 must handle the explicit Error for M3 worlds; this is safer than producing unloadable artifacts under the current package boundary.
requested_action: Please re-review the R2 save/error-model compatibility fix, focusing on explicit requestSaveV1 rejection for state.m3, absence of M3 eventTail emission without parser support, unchanged non-M3 save/load determinism, and allowed-path boundary compliance.
